heading: General Counsel
label: 1508 General Counsel
text: There shall be in the Department of Commerce a General Counsel, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.
notes: CodificationProvisions of section that fixed the compensation of the General Counsel have been omitted as the position is under the Executive Schedule under , Government Organization and Employees.section 5315 of Title 5Section was formerly classified to prior to the general revision and enactment of Title 5 by , , .section 592b of Title 5Pub. L. 89–55480 Stat. 378Sept. 6, 1966

Amendments1954—Act , amended section generally, establishing a General Counsel in the Department of Commerce and a rate of compensation. See Codification note above.Aug. 20, 19541952—Act , redesignated Solicitor as General Counsel and provided that “all laws and orders relating or referring to the Solicitor shall be deemed to relate or refer to the General Counsel”.July 17, 1952

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions of other officers, employees, and agencies of Department of Commerce, with certain exceptions, to Secretary of Commerce, with power to delegate, see Reorg. Order of SuccessionFor order of succession during any period when both Secretary and Deputy Secretary of Commerce are unable to perform functions and duties of office of Secretary, see Ex. Ord.
